rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Implementation-level contracts may only use assign for output features or declared auxiliary variables; guarantees are not allowed in implementation scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features; implementation contracts may reference subcomponents only in bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the input model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct syntax and valid identifiers.
Do not redeclare AADL ports as AGREE variables; they are already in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not use unresolved or invalid AADL property names as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of component structure
